quoteFirst: _ , July 24th: “Until further orders the guard in   the trenches by night, will be 2400 men, under a General of the day   with three Field Officers; of this number 600 men will work if   required by the Royal Engineers, from
quoteLast: , and return to their   camp at 8 a.m. if it should seem prudent to the senior Officer in the   trenches to dispense with them.... The remainder of the guard will   furnish working parties as usual during the day, when required by the   Royal Engineers.... There will be a special working party, consisting   of 400 men under a Field Officer, independent of the guard of the   trenches, except in case of an attack, when they will be available to   be called upon by the Officer commanding in the trenches.... General   Officers of divisions furnishing the guards in the trenches are to   consider the remainder of their division as a support ready to   reinforce
title: A History of the Coldstream Guards, from 1815 to 1895

quoteFirst: As before explained, the time from four to eight is divided into two short watches, and at eight o'clock a full watch begins, in which the hours are sounded the same as from noon to 4 P.M. This watch ends at midnight and is followed by another till 4 A.M.; from
quoteLast: is another, and from 8 A.M. till noon is another.
